termItem.definition: A GIS-integrated property valuation system that uses spatial analysis, comparable sales, and location attributes to estimate real estate values automatically.
The process of analyzing geospatial data to derive insights for site selection, market analysis, and property investment decisions in real estate.
A GIS-based metric measuring how easily residents can access amenities like shops, parks, and transit within walking distance, influencing property values.
GIS-based risk assessment for natural disasters (floods, hurricanes, wildfires) used by insurers to price policies and manage exposure.
Using GIS to identify properties within FEMA-designated flood zones, critical for underwriting flood insurance policies and calculating premiums.
A spatial analysis technique that identifies geographic risk factors for crime, accidents, or property damage, used in liability and property insurance.
Creating virtual geographic boundaries using GPS/beacons to trigger targeted mobile ads or notifications when customers enter a specific area.
Using GIS to define and analyze the geographic region from which a business draws its customers, optimizing store locations and marketing spend.
Grouping customers based on geographic and demographic data to tailor marketing campaigns, product offerings, and media buying strategies.

Urban planning approach using GIS to promote compact, transit-oriented, walkable communities while preserving natural resources.
Urban planning approach using GIS to promote compact, transit-oriented, walkable communities while preserving natural resources.
Urban planning approach using GIS to promote compact, transit-oriented, walkable communities while preserving natural resources.
GIS classification of land into categories like residential, commercial, agricultural, or forest, essential for urban growth management.
Using GIS route planning to optimize final delivery from distribution hubs to customers, reducing fuel costs and delivery times.
GIS modeling of travel time or distance from facilities to determine coverage zones, critical for warehouse and distribution network planning.
Using GIS to identify areas with high carbon storage potential, supporting environmental offset programs and sustainability reporting.
GIS assessment of how development breaks up natural habitats, used in environmental impact studies and conservation planning.
Combining machine learning with GIS for predictive analytics across real estate valuation, insurance risk, and customer behavior forecasting.
